steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by describing what the Federalist Papers are, then pivots with 'Though' to focus on authorship — what is certain and what is uncertain. The correct choice must capture both the collection's description and the authorship complexity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To summarize a contentious argument made in a collection of essays.’: focuses only on the arguments for ratification and omits any mention of authorship uncertainty. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To describe a collection of essays and address what is known about their authorship.’: describes the collection (85 essays by Hamilton, Jay, and Madison, published 1787–88, arguing for ratification) and addresses authorship (most essays have certain attribution, but some like No. 52 may be Hamilton's or Madison's). ✓
  • Choice C — ‘To point out that some essays in a notable collection were written by one person, while others were written by two people.’: centers on publication history rather than authorship. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To present the publication history of a collection of essays.’: misrepresents authorship as collaborative multi-author writing rather than uncertain single authorship. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
